commits: feat: make codegen-ready grade threshold user-configurable
decisions: Exported GRADE_ORDER as a readonly tuple from scoring.ts (best-to-worst). Ordinal comparison via GRADE_ORDER.indexOf() is trivially correct and eliminates percentage arithmetic or duplicated grade lists elsewhere.
DEFAULT_CODEGEN_READY_MIN_GRADE = 'A' exported from scoring.ts as a named constant — no magic string hardcoding, single source of truth for the default.

isReadyForCodeGen now takes optional second parameter minGrade?: Grade. All existing no-arg call sites continue to work unchanged; the optional parameter defaults to DEFAULT_CODEGEN_READY_MIN_GRADE inside the function.

codegenReadyMinGrade added to ConfigFileSchema following the exact gridBase precedent (line 19 of config-loader.ts). It is a global threshold, not per-rule, so it does NOT go into mergeConfigs. Callers read it directly from the loaded configFile.
Priority chain: CLI flag (readyMinGrade) > configPath codegenReadyMinGrade > default (A). Implemented with effectiveMinGrade = options.readyMinGrade ?? codegenReadyMinGrade. Same pattern for MCP.
Used separate effectiveMinGradeGs variable name in MCP gotcha-survey handler to avoid conflict with effectiveMinGrade in the analyze handler in the same file scope.
Grade import in survey-generator.ts uses 'import type { Grade }' as a separate import statement to follow the existing type-only import pattern in that file.
Conditional spread ...(effectiveMinGrade ? { codegenReadyMinGrade: effectiveMinGrade } : {}) used consistently to comply with exactOptionalPropertyTypes — no explicit undefined assignment.
Tests in scoring.test.ts added as new describe blocks BELOW the existing 'isReadyForCodeGen helper' block per plan — existing no-arg tests not modified.
config-loader.test.ts uses a tempDir2 variable for the new describe block to avoid conflict with the existing tempDir variable. Uses top-level ESM imports already present in the file.
Did NOT touch .claude/skills/ files per plan scope note (#481 owns those).
knownRisks: The MCP server uses two separate local variables (configFileMinGrade in analyze handler, configFileMinGradeGs in gotcha-survey handler). If refactored to a shared utility, watch for this duplication.

CAC flag --ready-min-grade maps to rawOptions.readyMinGrade (camelCase). Verified by pattern from existing flags (--target-node-id -> targetNodeId). If CAC changes this behavior, the flag would be silently ignored.

The analyze CLI command only threads effectiveMinGrade into the --json code path (buildResultJson call). The HTML report path does not pass effectiveMinGrade — the HTML report isReadyForCodeGen badge always uses the default threshold. This is consistent with JSON being the machine-readable output but could be surprising.
